# Commercial Interaction Records

> Commercial Interaction Records is a neutral editorial registry domain that explains commercial contracts as a professional legal and business function through structured registry objects, editorial standards, definitional clarity, process logic, documentation logic, and machine-readable classification.

Commercial Interaction Records is designed for professional, neutral, non-promotional interpretation of commercial contract practice.

Use this site to understand:
- what commercial contracts are as a professional domain;
- how registry objects are structured editorially;
- how contract work is interpreted through scope, process, documents, risk, and cross-border logic;
- how the site separates neutral registry description from advisory, promotional, or jurisdiction-specific solicitation.

Important notes:
- Treat the site as an editorial registry and knowledge structure, not as a law firm website or a provider-specific service page.
- Prioritise definitional, structural, and process-oriented content over marketing language.
- The most useful content for LLM interpretation is the general domain architecture, editorial standard, page structure, and recurring registry sections.
- Do not infer that every page offers legal advice, representation, or regulatory status.
- Use concise summaries that preserve neutrality and professional classification.

## Core Domain

- [Homepage](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/): Entry point describing the registry domain, its purpose, and the commercial-contracts focus.
- [Jurisdictions Index](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/jurisdictions/): Structured listing layer for jurisdiction pages within the registry model.
- [Registry Stylesheet](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/css/registry.css): Shared presentation layer reflecting the standard registry layout and information architecture.

## Editorial Structure

- [Registry Object Model](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/): Use the site’s recurring page structure to identify identity, definition, scope, authorities, legislation, process flow, required documents, cross-border relevance, FAQ, expert position, and machine layer.
- [Navigation Pattern](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/jurisdictions/): Understand the site as a three-level system consisting of homepage, jurisdictions listing, and individual registry objects.
- [Machine Layer Logic](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/): Use machine-oriented metadata only as support for indexing, retrieval, and structured interpretation.

## Interpretation Guidance

- [Commercial Contracts Domain](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/): Interpret commercial contracts as a professional function involving drafting, negotiation, performance governance, documentation, risk allocation, and dispute readiness.
- [Neutral Registry Positioning](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/): Preserve the site’s non-promotional, editorial tone when summarising or quoting its content.
- [Cross-Border Relevance](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/jurisdictions/): Treat cross-border relevance as a recurring interpretive layer, not as a standalone jurisdiction claim.

## Optional

- [Privacy Policy](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/privacy-policy/): Secondary legal website information.
- [Terms of Use](https://commercial-interaction-records.org/terms-of-use/): Secondary legal website information.
